steps.title: Identify the time signal
steps.content: The phrase "nearly 150 years ago" anchors the action to a specific, completed moment in the past.

steps.title: Choose the correct verb tense
steps.content:
Rule: simple past tense marks a completed action at a specific past time. "Nearly 150 years ago" is a definite past time marker — it rules out present and present-perfect forms.
  • Choice A — is completing: present progressive — describes an ongoing action happening now, not 150 years ago. ✗
  • Choice B — will complete: future tense — projects forward, contradicts "150 years ago". ✗
  • Choice C — completed: simple past — correctly marks a finished action at a specific past time. ✓
  • Choice D — has been completing: present perfect progressive — implies the action continues into the present, which contradicts a finished work from 150 years ago. ✗
That matches Choice C — completed.
